task_completion.description: Open the Export dialog in Ardour.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export' or 'Export to Audio File(s)' dialog box is visible on screen over the Ardour interface.

task_completion.description: Set the export format to WAV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the export dialog, the selected audio format is clearly set to 'WAV'.

task_completion.description: Set the bit depth to 16-bit or 24-bit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the export dialog, the sample format or bit depth setting is visible and set to either '16-bit' or '24-bit'.

task_completion.description: Set the export destination path to /home/ga/Audio/export/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The destination folder or output path in the export dialog shows '/home/ga/Audio/export/'.
An export progress bar is visible, or the export button is seen being clicked to process the audio.

task_completion.description: Verify the exported WAV file exists and is valid/non-silent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows a '.wav' file in '/home/ga/Audio/export/' with a file size greater than 0 bytes (e.g., size shown in KB or MB, indicating it contains audio data).

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Ardour's menus and export dialogs, rather than terminal commands to generate the audio file.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the export using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal commands like 'touch', 'cp', or 'mv' used to place a fake or pre-existing .wav file into the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual open Ardour session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour main window shows an active session with tracks and audio regions before the export dialog is opened.
